scan_pihole.xml: The device 192.168.0.5 (pi.hole) audit identifies several critical vulnerabilities. The scan reveals that the device is running OpenSSH version 8.4p1 which is known to be highly vulnerable to attacks especially on port 22. The dnsmasq service is non-standard for Linux DNS servers and could potentially expose the device to exploitation. Furthermore, an open RDP service on port 3389 makes the device a prime target for data breaches, risking credential theft. Actions: 1. Bind SSH to a non-standard port and implement fail2ban. 2. Configure dnsmasq to limit DNS queries to trusted IPs only. 3. Close port 3389 unless explicitly required.
laptop_scan.xml: The device 127.0.0.1 (localhost) audit identifies the presence of open ports that expose vulnerabilities. Port 5432 is associated with PostgreSQL version 9.6.0 which is non-standard for Linux DNS servers and highly vulnerable to brute-force attacks. These services are prime targets for data breaches and risking credential theft. Additionally, Ports 5000 and 7000 for AirPlay services increase the network attack surface. Actions: 1. Bind the PostgreSQL service to localhost only. 2. Configure strong authentication for the RTSP service. 3. Close all unnecessary ports to reduce the attack surface.
